son have sold to Mss Margaret NJ

ington Furniture Co. and covers the

In response to communication
from a city taxpayer. in which it is
asserted that the city can not leg
ally fix the hours of labor and the
pay on city work. Corporation Coun-
sci Conner cites the test of the
wage scale made in Broad case, in
which the right of the city to regu-
late wages was upheld by the SUI
preme court.


The surplus and undivided profits
Of che Spokane & Eastern Trust Co.
amount to $31S,000. or more than
its capital, which is 100000. The
surplus is 100000 and the undi
vided profits $11S,O00


Or. e. PittWood, who has recently
attained some notice in connection
with the effort to complete the
building of the Sprague avenue fill
announces that he is handling the
question with kid gloves. and does
not intend to rub the fur the wrong
way on either side of the contro-
yersy.


Walter kinn,an, a teleqrapher,
six feet four inches tall and weigh
ing 23S pounds. has applied to the
local recruiting station for admis-
sion into the signal corps AS 100
pounds Is the limit in weight al
lowed by the war department, an
had to be asked by wire from Wash
ineton.


Examinations Of applicants for
positions as census enumeratOrs
We're held today.


A meeting Of the West Side I'M
provement club will be held tonight
at the office of Finch & Campbell to
consider ways and means of fur
thering improvements in that sec
ton of the city


A letter from Vice President
Sewall of the Chicago Milwaukee &
Puget Sound road. written in reply
to the request of the chamber of
commerce that the company grant
Spokane terminal rates in view of
the granting of it's franchise here,
states that 1t will be impossible for
he Milwaukee to give this conccs-
Son. He further insists that it
would be improper for the road to
make any promises as to rates in
advance of the decision Of the Spo-
kane rate case by the interstate
commerce commission.


r. E. Burke, a striking switch
man, was arrested yesterday after.
noon by Patrolman Bradley and is
charged with carrying concealed
weapons. Burke was hiding a large
bolt under his coat, but denies that
he intended 1t for weapon, though
he will give no other satisfactory
explanation.


Eli Stosovich and Nick Radovich
were fined S. each and costs in the
police court yesterday afternoon.
Pete Sovich, the third of the "vich''
trio who was arrested for fighting,
was released. as he had got the
worst of the deal.


William Sharp, accused Of pur.
chasing a number of the hides
stolen from the Northern Pacific by
Everett Davie and John Margeln
was arrested by Detectives Nc,
Cloud and Hogan yesterday after.
noon. Judge Mann placed Sharp
on his own recognzance. The
youthful thieves were sent to the
reform school yesterday.
